{"key_event":"Mass suffrage and Jacksonian party mobilization","reasoning":"Civil society became stronger as white male suffrage widened, mass parties, newspapers, voluntary associations, debtor movements, and sectional mobilization expanded, though slavery, Native dispossession, and women’s exclusion kept this from being broadly inclusive dominance. State power also rose through Supreme Court nationalism, tariffs, federal land policy, foreign-policy assertion, and state-led infrastructure, but administrative and coercive reach remained only moderate by the rubric.","society_change":0.4,"society_power":7.3,"state_change":0.4,"state_power":5.8}
